title: Exploring Kubernetes
description: A progressive journey through Kubernetes, from your first deployment to production operations.

items.title: Deployments
items.content_html: How Kubernetes Deployments manage ReplicaSets to scale, self-heal, and roll out zero-downtime updates — the resource you actually create in production.

items.title: Jobs and CronJobs
items.content_html: Run tasks to completion instead of forever — database migrations, scheduled backups, and batch processing with Kubernetes Jobs and CronJobs.

items.title: Health Checks and Probes
items.content_html: Liveness, readiness, and startup probes — what each one actually controls, the HTTP/TCP/exec mechanisms behind them, and the classic mistake that turns a database hiccup into a crash loop.

items.title: ReplicaSets Under the Hood
items.content_html: What a ReplicaSet actually does, why Deployments manage them instead of Pods directly, and how to debug one when Pod counts don't match what you expect.

items.title: Resource Requests and Limits
items.content_html: Requests and limits explained the way they actually behave in the kernel — including why CPU limits throttle pods whose average usage graph looks perfectly fine.

items.title: The CRI: How kubelet Talks to Container Runtimes
items.content_html: How kubelet talks to containerd or CRI-O through the CRI: the interface, the dockershim history, and the full path from apply to a running container.

items.title: Cluster Architecture
items.content_html: A working mental model of a Kubernetes cluster — the control plane that decides, the nodes that run your Pods, and the reconcile loop that ties them together.

items.title: cert-manager: Certificates as Cluster Resources
items.content_html: Issuers, Certificates, and one Gateway annotation: how cert-manager turns TLS issuance into desired state — plus the debug chain for when it won't issue.

items.title: Pointing Your Domain at the Cluster with external-dns
items.content_html: The last manual step, automated: external-dns watches your HTTPRoutes and writes DNS records — to a cloud provider, or to authoritative DNS you run yourself.

items.title: Gateway API with Traefik: The Standard Front Door
items.content_html: Stop writing Ingress annotations. How Gateway API's role-split design works — GatewayClass, Gateway, HTTPRoute — wired end to end with Traefik, TLS included.

items.title: Kubernetes Ingress: Reading the Front Door You Inherit
items.content_html: Every existing cluster still routes through Ingress. Read rules, pathTypes, TLS, and annotations fluently — and plan the Gateway API migration off ingress-nginx.

items.title: LoadBalancer Services: From Cloud to Bare Metal
items.content_html: What actually happens when you set type: LoadBalancer — cloud controllers, MetalLB on bare metal, pending IPs, traffic policy, and the one-per-app trap.

items.title: Getting Helm Access
items.content_html: Install Helm, the Kubernetes package manager, and connect it to your cluster. Step-by-step guide for macOS, Linux, and Windows with troubleshooting tips.

items.title: Essential Helm Commands
items.content_html: Master daily Helm commands: list, status, get values, upgrade, rollback. Covers when to use Helm vs kubectl, plus a real-world incident rollback walkthrough.

items.title: Your First Helm Deployment
items.content_html: Deploy your first Helm chart to Kubernetes. Covers CI/CD pipeline charts and vendor charts with values.yaml customization, upgrades, rollbacks, and cleanup.

items.title: Understanding What Helm Created
items.content_html: See how Helm translates values.yaml into running Kubernetes resources. Understand the rendering pipeline, release tracking, and how to troubleshoot each layer.

items.title: Understanding What Happened
items.content_html: Deep dive into Kubernetes architecture. Learn how Deployments, ReplicaSets, and Pods work together when you deploy applications to K8s clusters.

items.title: Overview
items.content_html: Developer-focused introduction to Kubernetes. Learn what K8s is, deploy your first application, and master essential kubectl commands with confidence.

items.title: What Is Kubernetes?
items.content_html: An easy-to-understand introduction to Kubernetes (K8s) for beginners. Learn about container orchestration, why it exists, and the benefits of using it.

items.title: Getting kubectl Access
items.content_html: Install kubectl and configure Kubernetes cluster access using kubeconfig files. Complete setup guide for AWS, Azure, GCP, and on-premises clusters.
